description_long: This apartment building features a large, sloped terracotta-colored roof with a prominent central ridge and a secondary, raised section. The exterior walls are finished in a light, neutral color, punctuated by rows of evenly spaced rectangular windows with blue-tinted glass and subtle framing. The building is three stories tall, with the main entrance located at the center of the longer facade, accessible via a wide staircase and a small overhang. The structure is rectangular in plan, with a symmetrical window arrangement and no visible balconies. The overall design is functional and modern, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on practicality. The building appears to be constructed primarily from concrete and glass, with a tiled roof.
description: A three-story apartment building with a large terracotta-tiled roof, light-colored walls, and rows of evenly spaced windows. The main entrance is centrally located and accessed by a staircase.
description_view_0: This view shows the shorter side of the building with three rows of windows and the sloped roof's edge.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the main entrance with a staircase and overhang, as well as the full length of the windowed facade.
description_view_2: This view mirrors view 0, showing the opposite short side with similar window arrangements and roof details.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the back facade, featuring rows of windows but no visible entrance or architectural features.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in symmetrical, evenly spaced horizontal rows across each floor, with consistent alignment and grouping on all visible facades.
